reason: Response A has a distinctive minimalist, aphoristic style with short declarative sentences that build into longer observations — a hallmark of Haiku's prose voice. It avoids the formulaic essay structure (intro paragraph, bullet-point-like body paragraphs, conclusion) that Response B follows, and its moral argument feels more organically embedded rather than listed. Response B reads more like a competent imitation that checks expected boxes (wabi-sabi reference, antique dealers, restored houses) in a predictable order, whereas A arrives at wabi-sabi almost reluctantly, as a footnote to lived experience.
